evidence: analysis/analysis_test.py contains a local reimplementation of filter_and_compare() — this is a stale pre-migration artifact from before protein_analysis_utils.py existed; it was never removed after the canonical version was introduced

analysis/non_srp_filter.py and analysis/srp_filter.py each define count_domains() locally instead of importing it from protein_analysis_utils.py or analysis/tmd_count.py — these are unconsolidated copies left from before the utility module existed

analysis/Uniprot_TMD_search.py also defines count_domains() locally and calculate_cterm_distance() which duplicates calc_min_cterm_distance in protein_analysis_utils.py — the same migration incompleteness
No deprecated markers (@deprecated, DEPRECATED comments) or TODO/FIXME migration comments found anywhere in the codebase
No mixed JS/TS or old/new API patterns — this is a Python-only project

The hard-coded path 'C:/Users/Jake/Desktop/non_biogrid.tsv' in non_srp_filter.py and srp_filter.py appears to be a development-machine path that was never migrated to a configurable path or command-line argument

strengths: No active deprecated API patterns or compatibility shims found
No TODO/FIXME migration markers indicating tracked-but-incomplete work
The move to protein_analysis_utils.py as a central utility module is effectively complete for the core filter functions
incomplete_migration.issue_character: The incomplete migration is the consolidation of count_domains() and cterm_distance logic: these utility functions were centralized in protein_analysis_utils.py but the old local copies in analysis scripts were never removed, leaving the codebase with parallel old and new versions.
incomplete_migration.score_rationale: The migration to a shared utility module was largely completed for the core filter-and-compare workflow, but the lower-level utility functions (count_domains, cterm_distance) were not migrated, and a stale pre-migration script (analysis_test.py) was left in place. The hard-coded dev paths in filter scripts are also migration artifacts. These are real but bounded defects, placing the score in the upper-middle range.

issues.summary: count_domains() was not migrated to protein_analysis_utils.py; old local copies persist in three analysis scripts
evidence: Identical count_domains() function defined locally in non_srp_filter.py, srp_filter.py, Uniprot_TMD_search.py, and tmd_count.py
protein_analysis_utils.py has no count_domains function — the consolidation was started (calc_min_cterm_distance was added) but count_domains was not moved
issues.suggestion: Add count_domains() to protein_analysis_utils.py; remove local definitions; import from there in all four files

issues.summary: Hard-coded Windows dev paths in non_srp_filter.py and srp_filter.py were never migrated to configurable inputs
issues.suggestion: Replace hard-coded paths with argparse command-line arguments or environment variables, matching the pattern used in IDR/idr_analysis.py

root_causes: The migration to protein_analysis_utils.py was done incrementally — higher-level filter functions were moved first, lower-level helpers were not
Dev paths were committed during development and not cleaned up before the scripts were considered complete
